title: KS-gated original-data augmentation with domain flags
summary: Use the original F1 strategy dataset as extra training data only through feature-specific shift gates and explicit domain/sample-weight controls.
rationale: The deep-dive original-dataset notebooks report a target-rate gap and uneven feature drift: TyreLife, Position, and Position_Change are relatively stable, while LapNumber, RaceProgress, LapTime_Delta, and Stint are high-shift. Blind concatenation can import useful positives but also teach the model a different synthetic/original domain.
implementation_hint: Compute KS or adversarial-shift scores between competition train and original data for each shared feature. Add original rows with an `is_original` flag, lower sample weight for high-shift rows, and optionally mask or interaction-encode high-shift features with the domain flag. Compare full concat, stable-feature-only concat, and weighted concat under race/year grouped OOF.
expected_effect: Could improve positive-class coverage and rare strategy windows while reducing the leaderboard risk from original-vs-synthetic covariate shift.
risk: If the original target mechanism differs from the competition generator, even weighted rows can bias rank ordering; validation must separate true feature gains from domain memorization.
